Oregon Distribution System Planning

Pacific Power is committed to delivering affordable, reliable and sustainable energy to our customers.

Comprehensive distribution system planning plays a part in transforming the energy grid to benefit customers. 

En español

Distribution System Planning

Pacific Power is developing a Distribution System Plan (DSP) for its service area in Oregon as informed by Oregon Senate Bill 978 (2017) and Governor Brown’s Executive Order No. 20-04 that highlight the importance of exploring new expectations for the electric grid, the importance of clean energy, inclusivity and customer options. Pacific Power’s DSP will be developed as outlined by the Oregon Public Utility Commission's Guidelines for Distribution System Planning.

Public input process

Pacific Power has pursued an open and collaborative approach to involve state regulatory agencies, customers and other stakeholders in our Integrated Resource Plan development and the company will continue that approach for the Distribution System Plan. Pacific Power will be holding a series of public workshops to share information and provide an opportunity for the Oregon Public Utility Commission (OPUC) and public stakeholders to get a more complete picture of how Pacific Power manages and plans to modernize the distribution system, as well as to provide a venue for stakeholders to submit comments and ideas.

2026 Workshops

3/18/2026

10:00 a.m. - 12:00 p.m. Winter 2026 State Workshop (slides and recording) 

  • Give a preview of the 2026 DSP Filing
  • Near-Term Action Plan
  • Provide update on feedback received from last workshop
  • Share a status update on new DSP process

Previous workshops

2025 Workshops

11/14/2025

10 a.m. - 12 p.m. State Workshop (hybrid) (slides and recording) 

  • Distribution System Planning Overview and Update
  • DSP Study Area Selection and Study Process
  • Electric Vehicle and Distributed Energy Generation Forecasting
  • DSP Pilot Concepts
  • Electrical Load Data Analytical Tools

2024 Workshops

4/8/2024

11 a.m. - 1 p.m. PT; Pendleton Local Workshop #1 (hybrid) (slides) (diapositivas)

  • Utility and DSP Overview
  • Community Based Renewable Energy Pilot
  • Study Area Overview (Pendleton)
  • Forecasting & Preliminary Grid Needs
4/10/2024

11 a.m. - 1 p.m. PT; Wallowa County Local Workshop #1 (hybrid) (slides) (diapositivas)

  • Utility and DSP Overview
  • Community Based Renewable Energy Pilot
  • Study Area Overview (Wallowa County)
  • Forecasting & Preliminary Grid Needs

2023 Workshops

Public Workshop #1 – slides (diapositivas) and recording

February 2023
Prineville Local Workshop #1 – slides

April 2023
Upper Rogue Local Workshop #1 – slides and recording

April 2023
Upper Rogue Stakeholder Workshop #2 – slides and recording

August 2023
Prineville Stakeholder Workshop #2 – slides and recording

August 2023

2021-2022 Workshops

Public Workshop #1

May 2021   
Public Workshop #2 – Part 1 June 2021
Public Workshop #2 – Part 2 July 2021
Public Workshop #4 – Community Engagement Questions August 2021
Public Workshop #5 (formerly #3) September 2021
Public Workshop #6 October 2021
Public Workshop #7 January 2022
Public Workshop #8 - DSP Survey Results May 2022
Public Workshop #9 June 2022
Public Workshop #10 July 2022

Planning principles

  • Transparent and comprehensive data sets for customers, communities and stakeholders to evaluate and set priorities as we move to a clean, equitable energy future   
  • Robust community engagement 
  • Technology adoption 
  • Grid resilience

Distribution System Planning Report

2026 report

  • 2026 Oregon Distribution System Plan Report

Prior reports

  • Oregon Distribution System Plan Report – Part 1  (2021)
  • Oregon Distribution System Plan Report – Part 2 (2022)

Map viewer

This is a high level map showing certain information within our system including distributed generation, energy equity and incentive information, and reliability. This map will evolve over time.

Submit feedback or connect with us

To join the distribution list and participate in this process, or for questions regarding the information provided, please email us at DSP@PacifiCorp.com.
